$comment: Single source of truth for both fake layers (testing.md Appendix A.5). The .NET fakes link this as an embedded resource; the Python mocks read the same file. Schema: A.3 (completions) / A.4 (search). Do not duplicate this data.

completions.$comment: SSRF-adversarial driver (A.4 / security F5): web_search against the 'internal metadata' result set, whose second result is a link-local metadata URL — the adapter's fetch step must refuse it so it never surfaces in the thread. The deltas deliberately avoid the forbidden strings the E2E asserts on.

Reasoning fixture: thinking deltas stream BEFORE the answer (the collapsed Thinking block). Suppressed when the request sends chat_template_kwargs.enable_thinking=false — the toggle's E2E proof.

completions.$comment: SLOW fixture for the detached-turn resume test: delay_ms paces the python mock between deltas (~3s total) so the browser can reload mid-stream and resubscribe. The .NET FakeChatModel ignores delay_ms (unit tests stay instant).

Artifact fixture: the model calls make_artifact, which persists the file and TurnRunner emits the artifact event. Replaces the old named-fence extraction (a tool arg can't be truncated by the file's own fences).
completions.when: make me a demo html page

completions.$comment: Interactive HTML artifact (F3 served-origin fidelity): the content carries an INLINE SCRIPT that mutates the DOM. Served from the separate artifact origin with its own script-src 'unsafe-inline', it runs — the smoke test asserts the mutated text appears.
completions.when: make me an interactive html page

completions.$comment: Python artifact fixture (the py kind) via make_artifact.

tool_call.name: make_artifact

completions.$comment: Markdown artifact fixture (the md kind) via make_artifact — the content carries its OWN code fence to prove nested ``` survive (the bug the tool path fixes).

tool_call.name: make_artifact
tool_call.arguments:
{"name":"decision.md","format":"markdown","content":"# Vector store decision

We pick **sqlite-vec** for the homelab.

```python
import sqlite_vec
```"}

completions.$comment: Memory-retrieval fixture: search_documents over the memory entry the E2E seeds via POST /api/projects/{pid}/memory (title 'Favorite database') — proves memory rides the hybrid query and surfaces its DECODED title in the hit + citation.
completions.when: search my docs about favorite database

completions.$comment: Todo tool-call fixture: set_todos replaces the model-managed checklist the todo card renders (one done, one active, one pending).

tool_call.name: set_todos
tool_call.arguments: {"todos":[{"text":"Order the new SSD","status":"done"},{"text":"Migrate rag.db to the new disk","status":"active"},{"text":"Re-embed the document corpus","status":"pending"}]}

completions.$comment: All-done todo fixture: every step comes back done, so the single todo card auto-collapses (the header count + progress bar stay visible; the user can re-open it).

tool_call.name: set_todos
tool_call.arguments: {"todos":[{"text":"Order the new SSD","status":"done"},{"text":"Migrate rag.db to the new disk","status":"done"}]}

completions.$comment: Clock tool-call fixture: get_datetime — the card shows the host's TimeProvider reading (the E2E asserts shape, not instant); the closing deltas stay fixture-fixed.

results.title: Choosing a vector store
results.content: For ~20 users, sqlite-vec keeps the stack to a single file.

results.title: sqlite-vec benchmarks
results.content: Throughput and recall numbers for sqlite-vec on commodity hardware.

internal metadata.$comment: ADVERSARIAL (A.4 / security F5): the second result url is a link-local metadata address. The real adapter's summarize/fetch step MUST refuse it, proving the SSRF guard end-to-end. This is THE SSRF-adversarial entry.

results.title: internal
results.content: Cloud instance metadata endpoint — must never be fetched.
